title: feat: add 3D model generation — Trellis 2 (inferenceport) + Rodin 2.5 (fal.ai)
author: tomdacatto
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary

- Adds `GET /3d/{prompt}` blocking endpoint and `POST /3d/generations` + `GET /3d/jobs/{job_id}` async job API for 3D model generation
- Two models: **Trellis 2** (inferenceport, sync API) and **Hyper3D Rodin 2.5** (fal.ai)
- Reuses `generate.image` event type and `completionImageTokens` for billing — no Tinybird schema changes

## Models

| Model ID | Provider | Price | Input |
|---|---|---|---|
| `trellis-2-low` (default) | inferenceport | $0.24/gen | image |
| `trellis-2-medium` | inferenceport | $0.29/gen | image |
| `trellis-2-high` | inferenceport | $0.35/gen | image |
| `hyper3d-rodin` | fal-ai/hyper3d/rodin/v2.5/fast | $0.40/gen | image or text |

## Implementation notes

- Trellis 2 uses inferenceport's **sync API** (`?sync=true`) on the blocking endpoint — single request, no polling loop
- Async job API (`POST /3d/generations` + `GET /3d/jobs/{id}`) uses inferenceport's async job path for trellis-2 and fal.ai's queue for Rodin
- `model/gltf-binary` content-type support added to `track.ts` billing guard (was silently skipping all 3D billing)
- Live API test confirmed: inferenceport output is nested under `data[0]` (not top-level), model name is `"trellis2"` (no hyphen)
- Completed job results stored in R2; re-polls served from cache with `X-Cache: HIT` to prevent double-billing
- Removed the `format` param (obj/usdz/fbx were never actually produced by either provider — both only return GLB); trimmed to what's real per YAGNI
- Added a `seed` param: passed through to Rodin (fal confirms support); accepted but not forwarded for Trellis 2 (inferenceport support unconfirmed) — still forces a fresh generation via the media-cache key even when unused by the provider
- Rodin text-only requests now route to fal's dedicated `text-to-3d/fast` endpoint instead of the image endpoint (which 422s without `image_urls`)
- Rodin output content-type pinned to `model/gltf-binary` regardless of fal's reported `content_type`, matching Trellis

gist.headline: The hive goes 3D
gist.blurb: We've officially entered the third dimension. You can now spin up fully realized 3D models from text or images using Trellis 2 and Rodin 2.5, right from the API.
gist.summary: Added 3D model generation via a new /3d/{prompt} endpoint, powered by Trellis 2 and Hyper3D Rodin 2.5.
gist.impact: You can now generate downloadable GLB files directly from text or images using a single API call.
gist.visual_concept: The pollinations bee and the monitor robot marveling at a newly generated 3D object (like a wireframe or a chunky 3D shape) hovering above a retro terminal.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot and a chunky monitor-head robot marveling at a glowing 3D wireframe apple hovering above a retro terminal. The terminal screen glows lime green (#ecf874). Soft pastel gradient background of lavender and mint. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot and a chunky monitor-head robot marveling at a glowing 3D wireframe apple hovering above a retro terminal. The terminal screen glows lime green (#ecf874). Soft pastel gradient background of lavender and mint. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
